questions.question: A hiring model was trained mostly on data from one group and now favours that group. What is this an example of?

answers.text: Bias, largely inherited from unrepresentative training data

questions.feedback: Unfair skew from unrepresentative data is bias. Fix it with representative data, bias detection, and human oversight.

questions.question: Which AWS tool detects bias and helps explain model predictions?
questions.feedback: SageMaker Clarify handles bias detection and explainability. Rekognition is vision; Polly is text-to-speech.

questions.question: A company wants its Bedrock chatbot to refuse disallowed topics and redact personal information. What should it use?
questions.feedback: Guardrails filter harmful content, block denied topics, and redact sensitive data. Model Monitor watches for drift; Textract reads documents.

questions.question: Which are sound responsible-AI practices?

answers.text: Keep a human in the loop for high-stakes decisions (e.g. Amazon A2I)
Use representative data and check for bias
Be transparent that AI is being used and about its limits
Fully automate consequential decisions with no oversight or explainability

questions.feedback: Human oversight, representative data, bias checks, and transparency are core. Fully automating high-stakes decisions with no oversight is the opposite of responsible AI.
